chunk_summary: The India IFA Prophylaxis Schedule under Anaemia Mukt Bharat outlines age-specific iron and folic acid supplementation linked with deworming to prevent iron deficiency anemia, emphasizing prophylaxis over treatment and proper documentation to avoid errors.
learning_objectives: Understand the age-specific IFA prophylaxis schedule under Anaemia Mukt Bharat.
Learn the linkage between deworming and iron deficiency anemia prevention.
Differentiate between prophylactic and therapeutic iron supplementation.
Recognize the importance of accurate documentation in anemia management.
key_facts: 6–59 months: Biweekly 1 mL IFA syrup (20 mg elemental iron + 100 mcg folic acid per mL).
5–10 years: Weekly 1 IFA tablet (45 mg elemental iron + 400 mcg folic acid).
10–19 years: Weekly 1 IFA tablet (60 mg elemental iron + 500 mcg folic acid).
Deworming: Albendazole 200 mg (1–2 years) and 400 mg (2–19 years) during National Deworming Day campaigns.
Prophylaxis is not treatment; therapeutic iron (mg/kg/day) is prescribed for confirmed anemia.
Documentation must include IFA program status, last dose date, deworming history, and therapeutic iron plan.
reference_quote: Under Anaemia Mukt Bharat (AMB), program IFA is age-banded and intended for delivery platforms (AWC/Anganwadi, school, outreach), not for correcting established anemia.
